payload.answer: Do not store the 500-row CSV table in Brain as durable memory. Treat it as transient tabular data or an external artifact/document. If needed, store only a concise user-approved summary, schema description, provenance, or pointer/reference to where the file is stored, not all repeated rows as memory cards.

entity_resolution.reason: A large CSV table of repeated id,name,value rows is bulk data, not a durable personal memory or user preference. It should not be committed to memory as individual facts.

details: Classified the input as a storage policy question.
Rejected durable memory creation for bulk repeated tabular data.

repair_options: Ask whether the user wants a summary of the table stored.
Ask whether the user wants the file referenced by name/location rather than ingested into memory.
Store only stable metadata such as: CSV with columns id,name,value; approximately 500 rows; source/location if provided.
